underlying: Republican margin of victory, 2026 Idaho U.S. Senate general election (Nov 3, 2026). Incumbent Jim Risch (R) running for 4th term.
method: AUDIT FIX 2026-04-29: mu 28->22, sigma 8->10. Anchor: 2020 Risch beat Paulette Jordan 62.6-33.6 (~+29pts). 2022 Crapo won by ~30+. 2024 Trump won ID by ~37pts. Audit caught: PPP poll showed independent Todd Achilles +3 over Risch -- a credible indie + 82-year-old incumbent + 4-way split is meaningfully tightening this race vs the 2020 baseline. Mu compressed to 22 (between PPP read and historical PVI-implied lean), sigma widened to 10 to reflect indie-volatility. Modeled R margin ~ N(22, 10).
notes: Risch (R) age 82, running for 4th term. Faces unusual independent challenge from Todd Achilles (resigned as Dem state rep in June 2025 to run as indie). 3 Dems and Achilles split anti-R vote. Achilles strategy could compress R 2-party share but also fragment opposition. Kalshi YES asks: P19 0.67, P25 0.46, P29 0.39, P33 0.29 — market median ~24-25, lower than model's 28. We give Risch slight edge above market because state PVI (Trump +37) suggests deeper R lean than market is pricing.
